Article Title: 'If someone dies .... ': Delhi Police probes alleged chats ahead of CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march
Article Text:
Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) supporters during a protest march, at Sansad Marg, in New Delhi (Picture credit: PTI)

Chats allegedly discussed communication during internet shutdown

Police examining messages on 'sacrifice' and mobilisation

Multiple FIRs registered after violence

NEW DELHI: Delhi Police is examining alleged chat messages exchanged among participants of the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) 'Sansad Chalo' march that turned violent on Monday, with some conversations suggesting preparations to maintain communication during a possible internet shutdown and others referring to sacrificing lives for the cause.Police sources cited by news agency PTI said on Tuesday that investigators were verifying the authenticity of the chats and the identities of those involved before drawing any conclusions.The alleged messages are being analysed along with CCTV footage, drone visuals, mobile phone videos and other electronic evidence collected during the investigation into the clashes, which left 118 police personnel and around 70 protesters injured, besides damaging 15-20 government vehicles.According to PTI, some of the alleged messages discussed alternative ways to stay connected if internet services were disrupted during the protest."After reaching the protest site you might not get network in your device. Police last time also used network jammers and this time too can do so. However, it is very important to stay connected to the group you came with," one of the alleged messages read.Investigators said participants had also shared links to applications that could reportedly function using Bluetooth signals in the absence of internet connectivity."Make sure all of your group have the same app installed and are already connected before entering the protest site," another alleged message stated.Police said they were examining the applications mentioned in the chats and would take action based on the findings.The alleged conversations also contained politically charged remarks and mobilisation messages.One participant claimed the following day would be the "last day of Modi Government."Some messages also referred to the possibility of participants sacrificing their lives during the protest."Dekho yarr jo jaan dene tak ko taiyaar woh ye sunlo ke kam se kam tumhara ehsan to rahega aane wali pidiyon par, aane wale bacho par (if someone died, future generations would remember such sacrifice)," one alleged message read.Another message stated, "Bhai agar kisi ki maut hoti hai to usme to sarkar pe zyada pressure aayega (if someone dies, then it will create a lot of pressure on the government)."Police said investigators were examining the context in which these remarks were made and were trying to determine whether they reflected personal opinions exchanged among participants or were part of any coordinated effort linked to the violence.Forensic examination of the digital communications is underway, with cyber experts assisting in establishing the origin, circulation and authenticity of the messages."Every message was being subjected to technical verification before being treated as evidence," a police source said, adding that the investigation remained evidence-based and further legal action would depend on corroborative material.The alleged chats surfaced as Delhi Police expanded its probe into the violence during the 'Sansad Chalo' march.Police have so far registered four FIRs and are preparing two more in connection with the violence, vandalism, alleged conspiracy and other incidents linked to the protest.The cases, registered at Parliament Street, Connaught Place and other police stations, invoke provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), the Arms Act and the Prevention of Damage to Public Property Act.Investigators are also examining CCTV footage, drone recordings, mobile phone videos, social media posts and other digital evidence to identify those involved in the clashes.Following the violence, security has been tightened across the national capital with additional police and paramilitary personnel deployed at sensitive locations, government installations and major intersections.
Article Summary: Delhi police is examining alleged chat messages exchanged among participants of the cockroach janta party's 'sansad chalo' march that turned violent on monday. Some conversations suggest preparations to maintain communication during a possible internet shutdown and others refer to sacrificing lives for the cause.

Article Title: Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day announced: Release date, skins and more
Article Text:
Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day collaboration teased (Image via Fortnite and Sony)

Epic Games releases teaser for Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day collaboration

When does the Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day collaboration release?

What skins could be included for Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day?

Will Web Shooters return?

Spider-Man Sprite in Fortnite

Spider-Man is officially swinging back into Fortnite, and Epic Games has confirmed it with a new teaser that has the community buzzing. The Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day collaboration has been officially teased on the game's social media channels, giving players their first real look at what is coming.The collaboration is set to be a part of the game’s upcoming update, which also aligns with the release date of the upcoming Spider-Man movie. The crossover will result in the introduction of themed skins, a new Sprite and even potential map changes.Here are further details about the Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day collaborationEpic Games shared a short video on the official Fortnite social media channels showing The Battlewoods POI covered in Spider-Man murals and Spidey's signature webs scattered across the location.The caption accompanying the post read: "These webs look kinda familiar."The tease is a clear and deliberate nod toward the return of the iconic Spider-Man Web Shooters, and potentially points to The Battlewoods becoming a central location for the collaboration.All of the content related to the Fortnite Brand New Day collab is expected to release on July 30, 2026, with the next Fortnite update. The timing is no coincidence, as Spider-Man: Brand New Day hits cinemas globally on July 31, 2026, meaning the collaboration drops on the island the day before the film opens worldwide.Epic has not yet officially revealed the full cosmetic lineup for the collaboration. However, based on leaks and the nature of the Brand New Day film, a new Spider-Man outfit inspired by the movie's suit design is the most obvious and certain inclusion.Along with the obvious new Spider-Man skin, players might finally see the addition of Jon Bernthal's Punisher and maybe even a new Hulk skin to the game.The teaser all but confirms it. The webs draped across The Battlewoods POI and the caption's knowing reference signals that the Mythic Web Shooters are making their return. The original Web Shooters from Chapter 3 Season 1 famously crashed Fortnite's servers within an hour of going live due to overwhelming player demand.Apart from the skins and map changes, Epic could finally release the leaked Spider-Man Sprite to the game, which reportedly will also allow players to use Spider-Man's Web Shooters to web swing.Data miner Blortzen previously revealed gameplay tags under "Sprite Net" that suggest the Spider-Man Sprite will introduce a web-swinging movement mechanic, making it one of the most mechanically unique Sprites in the entire Chapter 7 Season 3 roster.With the official teaser now live and July 30 confirmed as the release window, the countdown to one of the most anticipated collaborations of the season has officially begun.This was all you needed to know about Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day.
Article Summary: The fortnite x spider-man brand new day collaboration has been officially teased on the game's social media channels. The collaboration is set to be a part of the game’s upcoming update, which also aligns with the release date of the upcomingspider-man movie.

Article Title: "She felt violated": Taylor Swift's alleged feelings over a private fallout may explain why Blake Lively missed her wedding to Travis Kelce
Article Text:
Taylor Swift felt a certain way about what Blake Lively did.(Image via Getty Images)

Taylor Swift’s reason for not inviting Blake Lively to her wedding to Travis Kelce might be rooted in how she felt

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce remain deeply in love with each other as many wait for their wedding pictures to be released

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce 's high profile wedding might have sparked chaos across as they tied the knot two weeks ago, but the way they have stayed away from inviting Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds to it, has continued to dominate headlines. But it seems the reason why Taylor Swift never invited her former best friends might be linked to a controversial reason and her emotions concerning it.In a report by Rob Shuter’s Naughty but Nice, a source close to the billionaire pop star Taylor Swift revealed that the way her private texts with Blake Lively were leaked, it made her feel “exposed and violated”.The source added, “That crossed a line for her.As per Rob Shuter’s Naughty but Nice, the source continued, ”There’s been an emotional gap for a long time…Rebuilding trust doesn’t feel possible right now.”While Taylor Swift became more and more public about the life she shares with Travis Kelce in the last few years, she has completely stayed away from speaking about Blake Lively in public.Interestingly, just a month before Blake Lively filed a lawsuit against Justin Baldoni, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce were spotted out on a dinner date in New York City with her and Ryan Reynolds.At this point in time, all eyes remain on how Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are maintaining secrecy about their new lives.As billions of fans wait for their wedding pictures to be released, Taylor Swift’s confession on how supportive Travis Kelce has resurfaced.Last year, Taylor Swift said, "As soon as I’d come back from Sweden I’d just play it for him, and I knew that this was the kind of album he was going to love the most…He’s so supportive of all of it, but he’s a real vibes guy."The billionaire pop star added, “All of my guy friends are like, ‘I just want Travis to pick me up and throw me over the roof, or throw me in the pool.’ ‘Cause it’s like, you know he’s 6’6”, but it’s also the width of the shoulders…Seeing him walk through a door is genuinely a privilege."While the wedding seemed chaotic, there are no confirmations when or if Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will be releasing their pictures from the ceremony.Though many had expected them to release it within hours of getting married, it has been close to three weeks since they got married and nothing has been released yet.
Article Summary: Taylor swift’s reason for not inviting blake lively to her wedding to travis kelce might be rooted in how she felt about her former best friends. A source close to the billionaire pop star taylor swift revealed that the way her private texts were leaked, it made her feel “exposed and violated”

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Buffalo Bills linked to Carolina Panthers' $4 Million WR in proposed deal
Article Text:
The Buffalo Bills have been linked with a potential trade for Carolina Panthers wide receiver Xavier Legette, with one analyst proposing a deal involving a 2027 fourth-round pick. The former first-round pick has yet to meet expectations but remains an intriguing buy-low option. His affordable rookie contract and athletic upside could strengthen Buffalo's receiving corps, while Carolina would gain additional draft capital for future roster building.

Category Details Player Xavier Legette Current Team Carolina Panthers Rumored Team Buffalo Bills Contract Status Rookie contract (entering third NFL season) Salary Cap Hit Rookie deal (not publicly changed) Trade Likelihood Moderate Latest Insider Update FanSided's Justin Carter proposed Buffalo as a landing spot Potential Return 2027 fourth-round pick

Which teams are interested in Xavier Legette?

What insiders are saying about the trade rumors

“The Buffalo Bills still need help at wide receiver, as they head into 2026 with Joshua Palmer set to play a key role on the roster. That is … not the best situation to be in, and while the addition of DJ Moore helps, it’s still clear that the team has an issue here. This proposed trade is actually one of two things I’d do if I were Bills GM Brandon Beane. First is this move: dealing a mid-round pick for Panthers wide receiver Xavier Legette, who hasn’t really panned out in Carolina but who has the skill to be a downfield threat with Josh Allen throwing the football.

“The other part of this? Recoup some draft capital by then turning around and trading Keon Coleman to a rebuilding team. I don’t think Carolina will want Coleman (hence why he isn’t in this deal), but essentially I’m thinking about what’s basically a three-team swap that lands Buffalo a more promising young wideout than the one that clearly isn’t working out.”

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the trade could impact both teams

The Buffalo Bills have already strengthened their receiving corps by acquiring DJ Moore, but trade speculation suggests they may not be finished. NFL analyst Justin Carter has proposed a deal that would send Carolina Panthers wide receiver Xavier Legette to Buffalo in exchange for a 2027 fourth-round pick, giving Josh Allen another young target ahead of the 2026 season.At the moment, Buffalo has emerged as one of the most discussed landing spots for Legette, even though no formal trade talks have been reported. The idea centers on the Bills' desire to add another athletic receiver behind DJ Moore while continuing to build around quarterback Josh Allen.Legette entered the NFL as a first-round selection in 2024 and was once considered a player Buffalo could target during that draft. Instead, the Bills traded back before selecting Keon Coleman. Two seasons later, both receivers have struggled to consistently produce, making a fresh start a logical talking point.Legette finished last season with 35 catches for 363 yards and three touchdowns after posting 49 receptions for 497 yards and four scores as a rookie. While those numbers have not matched expectations, Buffalo could view him as a low-risk addition with untapped upside.FanSided's Justin Carter believes Buffalo should take advantage of Legette's declining value while also reshaping its receiving room.The proposal remains speculative, but it reflects a belief that Buffalo could benefit from another receiver capable of stretching defenses.Legette is still playing under his rookie contract, making him an affordable trade target. There is no reported no-trade clause attached to his deal, and his contract gives Carolina flexibility if it decides to move on.For Buffalo, adding a player on a rookie-scale contract would provide depth without placing significant pressure on the salary cap. That financial flexibility could prove valuable as the Bills continue building around several high-priced veterans.For Buffalo, Legette would likely compete for snaps behind DJ Moore, Khalil Shakir and Joshua Palmer while pushing Keon Coleman for playing time. His speed could add another vertical option for Josh Allen, especially if the Bills believe a different environment can unlock his potential.Carolina, meanwhile, would gain additional draft capital that could help strengthen other areas of the roster. If the Panthers have already begun looking beyond Legette as a long-term answer, a mid-round pick could offer better value than waiting for another inconsistent season.
Article Summary: The buffalo bills have been linked with a potential trade for carolina panthers wide receiver xavier legette. Fansided's justin carter proposed a deal involving a 2027 fourth-round pick. The bills have already strengthened their receiving corps by acquiring dj moore, but trade speculation suggests they may not be finished.

Article Title: "Wild pursuit of fame and fortune": Candace Owens tears into Erika Kirk, claims Charlie Kirk was her "ticket to more fame"
Article Text:
Candace Owens intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, accusing her of pursuing fame and fortune throughout her life. In a podcast, Owens claimed Charlie Kirk was Erika's "ticket to more fame" and argued a recent article criticizing her instead exposed Erika's character. She also questioned Erika's motives, saying her focus had always been on public attention, money, and influence.

Candace Owens responds to article about Erika Kirk with blistering remarks



Candace Owens claims Erika Kirk pursued fame throughout her life

Candace Owens has escalated her public feud with Erika Kirk after reacting to a recent article that discussed their fractured relationship. A screenshot from Owens' latest podcast episode quickly spread across social media, highlighting her sharp criticism of Charlie Kirk 's widow and reigniting debate over the fallout that has dominated conservative circles in recent weeks.The image circulating online shows Owens addressing the article line by line before launching into a lengthy critique of Erika Kirk's motivations. During the episode, Owens argued that the story unintentionally revealed more about Erika than it did about her.She said:"I have to tell you, it is the way this article concludes that is just stunning to me. It continues, quote, Erika says she's not going to give that woman an ounce of her energy. It looks like I'm getting a lot of it right now, but sometimes she just can't help herself and she'll just sort of explode the tirade.""She hates her, Candace, more than she hates her political opponents. Who are your political opponents, Erica? Who are your political opponents? Who are you? Who are you? You're some beauty pageant wife who took over the reins of Charlie's company, telling us a story that he wanted it in the end, but just couldn't put it in documents.""Who are you to think that you have political opponents? It continues, at least with them, she knew where she stood. They didn't exactly betray her like Candace did. Candace did her so dirty. I think at this point, Erika likes even Tyler Robinson more than she likes Candace. End quote."Owens then claimed the article failed in its apparent attempt to damage her reputation, arguing that readers instead focused on Erika Kirk's actions and public image.Owens went further by accusing Erika Kirk of seeking public attention long before marrying Charlie Kirk. She questioned her past choices and suggested that fame, rather than principle, had driven many of her decisions.Continuing her remarks, Owens said:"Yeah, Erika, that's what Druski and everybody who's been speaking about this has been getting at. That's why people think there's something wrong with you, because you meant this article to be a smackdown of me, but all it did was further reveal your character.""You have been on a wild pursuit of fame and fortune your entire life. You started doing pageants. I think we showed when you were like 12, okay? Sixth grade, maybe. And you have not stopped wanting to be in the spotlight. Every person you have dated has had some degree of fame, and you saw Charlie as your ticket to more fame.""And then you finally got it in your view when you walked out in a white pantsuit with sparklers behind you, and you sucked all of that in. That was it. That was the moment. We look back at those photos of people at the memorial. I have never seen such glee and such happiness. You clinked glasses. You guys then went to a restaurant. You celebrated. Finally, Erica, finally for you.""After decades of trying, you had made it into the spotlight, and everybody loved you. Brand, merchandising, shirts. You got on that Zoom call. The sales have been crazy. 50,000 shirts sold and kicked back into the LLC that me and the Charlie Kirk show people own for Charlie, for Charlie. And then it all came crashing down because maybe Charlie had an actual friend. That's what's annoying. Me, I'm the renegade. I'm the one thing. ""He had an actual friend who wasn't content to sell a T-shirt and move on and then sell some fed slop and move on because the price was right. That's what you're upset about, Erika, money and fame. There was no substance in this article because there was no substance in you. And that's my opinion. Not a call for you to get hurt, but me demonstrating that you have a character flaw that you are not even aware of."The latest comments add another chapter to the increasingly personal dispute between Owens and Erika Kirk. While Owens framed her remarks as her opinion, the exchange has continued to fuel intense reactions online, with supporters and critics on both sides debating the claims made during the podcast.
Article Summary: Candace owens has escalated her public feud with erika kirk after reacting to a recent article that discussed their fractured relationship. A screenshot from owens' latest podcast episode quickly spread across social media, highlighting her sharp criticism of charlie kirk's widow and reigniting debate over the fallout that has dominated conservative circles.

Article Title: Despite 35 minute delay Vikram-1 keeps date with Indian space history with Mission Aagaman success
Article Text: SRIHARIKOTA: After some anxious moments when the automatic launch sequence of Vikram-1 was halted midway at just five minutes before lift-off, Skyroot Aerospace finally created history after it successfully placed India’s first privately developed orbital-class rocket into space.The seven-storey tall Vikram-1 rocket, with a lift-off weight of 45,000 kg, blasted off at 12.05 pm, a good 35 minutes behind the originally scheduled time, from the first launch pad at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre-Sriharikota Range on its maiden orbital test flight, Mission Aagaman.The vehicle completed its planned flight sequence and placed its payloads into the targeted low Earth orbit, validating key systems developed by the Hyderabad-based startup.Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke personally with the Skyroot founders Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka on phone and congratulated the duo on the successful completion of Mission Aagaman.The PM, who sent a postcard into space abroad Vikram-1 with Vande Mataram written on it, pointed out to the Skyroot team that the feat was also momentous coming as it does when Vande Mataram is commemorating 150 years.The feat marks a major milestone for the country’s commercial launch sector and opens a new chapter at Sriharikota 46 years after ISRO’s first successfully launched India’s first launch vehicle SLV-3 into orbit.The launch came exactly 46 years after ISRO’s SLV-3E2 placed the Rohini satellite RS-1 into orbit on July 18, 1980, making India the sixth nation capable of launching satellites independently. Like SLV-3, Vikram-1 stands 22 metres tall, but its success represents a different leap: the arrival of private Indian industry in orbital launch operations.Built with an all-carbon composite structure, Vikram-1 uses in-house propulsion systems, including high-thrust solid-fuel stages and 3D-printed engines. The mission targeted a 450 km orbit at an inclination of 60 degrees and lasted just under 18 minutes.The rocket carried a mix of demonstration and symbolic payloads, including Skyroot’s SCOPE payload, SOLARAS, a 1U CubeSat from Karnataka-based Grahaa Space, Embrace, an in-orbit robotic arm demonstration from Telangana-based Cosmoserve Space, and payloads from Germany’s DCubed GmbH.It also carried a handwritten postcard from Prime Minister Narendra Modi bearing the words “Vande Mataram”, along with hundreds of letters from Skyroot’s team and supporters.In attendance at the historic launch were astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla, Andhra Pradesh IT minister Nara Lokesh, with his son in tow, in addition to ISRO chairman V Narayanan, IN-SPACe chairman Pawan Goenka and former ISRO director S Somanath.Founded in 2018 by former ISRO scientists Chandana and Daka, Skyroot had earlier made history on Nov 18, 2022 when Vikram-S became the first privately built Indian rocket to reach space on a suborbital flight. With Aagaman, the company has now moved from demonstration to orbital capability.
Article Summary: Skyroot aerospace successfully launches india's first privately developed orbital-class rocket into space. The seven-storey tall vikram-1 rocket blasted off at 12.05 pm, a good 35 minutes behind the originally scheduled time. Prime minister narendra modi spoke personally with the skyroot founders pawan kumar chandana and naga bharath daka on phone.

Article Title: Larry Ellison, Ken Griffin, Dace Brown Stubbs and other billionaires are buying neighboring houses using 'methods' called 'Landmaxxing', 'Defensive buying' and ...
Article Text:
Larry Ellison

How and why Larry Elisson, Jeff Bezos and other billionaires are expanding their compounds



Larry Ellison and several other billionaires may be increasingly buying neighbouring homes and land to create larger private compounds. A report claims that the Oracle founder, hedge fund billionaire Ken Griffin, Jack Daniel's heiress Dace Brown Stubbs and several other wealthy buyers are doing this. According to Coldwell Banker's midyear luxury market report, the trend has given rise to terms such as "Landmaxxing", which means acquiring additional land to expand a property, and "defensive buying," where buyers purchase adjacent land to preserve privacy or protect views.Citing the report, The Wall Street Journal noted that searches for US. luxury properties doubled in the first five months of 2026 compared to the same period in 2025. Searches for buildable land jumped 97% year-over-year, and searches for private islands and estates more than doubled, indicating rising demand for larger, multi-parcel properties.Among the buyers highlighted in the report is Citadel founder Ken Griffin, who has spent more than $450 million assembling a 27-acre compound in Palm Beach. Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has purchased properties worth more than $230 million on Miami's Indian Creek Island, while Oracle founder Larry Ellison has continued acquiring neighbouring properties in Malibu, California, Incline Village, Nevada, and Manalapan, Florida.A real estate agent named Danny Hertzberg working for Coldwell Banker Realty said many buyers now see adjacent properties as both lifestyle assets and long-term investments."This is more sought-after than the 40,000-square-foot house. For some people, it is privacy and security. For others, it is a hedge against inflation,” he said.He added that neighbouring property owners are often the first buyers approached when a home goes on sale because they are willing to pay more to expand their holdings.Real estate agents say buyers are using neighbouring properties for a range of purposes, including guest accommodation, staff housing, gardens, boating facilities, gyms, spas and sports courts.Venture capitalist Benjamin Ling expanded his holdings on Miami Beach's Palm Island by purchasing the waterfront property next to his home. Investor Ibrahim Al-Rashid also bought two neighbouring homes on Hibiscus Island, later building a guesthouse with a gym, spa and a man-made fish pond alongside his primary residence.Businessman Patrick Dovigi purchased three nearby parcels last year, with plans to add a gym, spa and cabana next to his waterfront home, along with a padel court on another lot across the street.Some buyers are also engaging in what Hertzberg described as "defensive buying," purchasing adjacent land to create a buffer between themselves and future developments.Dace Brown Stubbs, whose family founded the company behind Jack Daniel's, said she expanded her Colorado ranch after concerns that new construction could alter the surrounding landscape."I hated the thought of a giant house or two being built on these beautiful fields,” she said.Stubbs initially purchased a 14.26-acre ranch and later spent an additional $1.15 million over several years to acquire another 40 acres to preserve the property's views.The report also highlighted WeatherTech founder David MacNeil, who has acquired multiple adjacent parcels in Manalapan, Florida. One nearby parcel is also being purchased by Larry Ellison, further expanding the Oracle founder's existing compound in the area.According to Coldwell Banker, average luxury single-family home sales volume increased 2.8% year over year between January and May, while the median luxury home price rose 4.7% to $1.83 million. The brokerage said the luxury market has continued to be driven largely by cash buyers and long-term wealth creation rather than mortgage rates.
Article Summary: Larry ellison, jeff bezos and other billionaires are expanding their compounds. The trend has given rise to terms such as "landmaxxing" and "defensive buying" searches for us. Luxury properties doubled in the first five months of 2026 compared to the same period in 2025.

Article Title: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis to everyone fearing AI replacing them: Look at what we humans have built around us, it is ...
Article Text:
Demis Hassabis said ‘We don’t have long to prepare’

A divided AI community

Demis Hassabis suggests US-lead watchdog for AI companies across the world

Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis has once again predicted that artificial intelligence is close at hand, telling an audience at Stanford Graduate School of Business that AGI could arrive around 2030, plus or minus a year. He called it an “enormous transformative technology” that will usher in “a new human era.” Hassabis also added that a decade from now, society will look back and realise it was “standing in the foothills of the singularity” — the moment when AI surpasses human intelligence and begins improving itself autonomously.Hassabis has warned that society must prepare for the profound implications of AGI. With AI already automating daily tasks and coding work, fears of job displacement are rising. AGI — capable of reasoning, decision‑making and adaptation — could take on far more complex responsibilities.But Hassabis also emphasized that certain human qualities will become even more valuable. In an interview with The Rundown AI’s Rowan Cheung, he said people with “taste, design sensibility, original thinking” and the ability to synthesize ideas across disciplines will be in a strong position over the next five years.“Look at what we built around us”To those worried about being replaced, Hassabis offered a reminder of human ingenuity:“We’re general intelligences ourselves, don’t forget. Look at what we built around us — it’s incredible — with our hunter‑gatherer brains. Why would we stop here?” He said humans are “instantly adaptable,” and that AGI will unlock “amazing new things” rather than halt human creativity.Along with this, the Google DeepMind CEO also provided a timeline that sits among a wide range of predictions from other AI leaders:* Sam Altman has said humanity is close to building digital superintelligence.* Dario Amodei predicted powerful AI could emerge as early as late 2026.* Jack Clark and Marina Favaro wrote that by 2027, AI systems could complete tasks that take humans weeks.* Yann LeCun has dismissed the concept of general intelligence as “complete BS,” arguing current transformer models cannot reach human‑level cognition.* DeepMind’s chief AGI scientist Shane Legg estimates a 50% chance of “minimal AGI” by 2028.Recently, Google DeepMind CEO argued it's time someone started policing it—and that the United States should hold the badge. In a manifesto titled "A Framework for Frontier AI and the Dawning of a New Age," the Nobel laureate laid out his most concrete regulatory pitch yet: a new standards body empowered to test the world's most advanced models before they ship, and, if the risk demanded it, to coordinate an industry-wide slowdown.Hassabis is specific about the shape of the thing. A conventional government agency, he argues, "would not be able to move fast enough, or have the right resources." So instead he wants a public-private body modelled on the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority—the self-regulator that oversees US brokers and markets. Fund it largely through industry, staff it with independent technical experts and open-source representatives, and keep it answerable to the federal government.The mechanics are elegant, which Hassabis knows. A model gets tagged "frontier-class" once it clears certain benchmark thresholds, and its maker becomes a "frontier lab" carrying extra responsibilities. That neatly sidesteps the endless fight over whether academic or open-source systems should be regulated—capability is the only thing that counts, not who built it or whether the code is public. Labs would share models with the body up to 30 days before release, voluntarily at first, before the review calcifies into a hard requirement for the US market.
Article Summary: Google deepmind ceo demis hassabis says agi could arrive around 2030. He says society must prepare for the profound implications of agi. Hassabis suggests us-lead watchdog for ai companies across the world. He said people with “taste, design sensibility, original thinking” will be in a strong position over the next five years.

Article Title: Oracle founder Larry Ellison wanted to buy Apple and fire its CEO, but Steve Jobs stopped him said: I do not need any…
Article Text:
Larry Ellison had $5 billion lined up to buy Apple and make Steve Jobs CEO—but Jobs said no

Larry Ellison had the $5 billion Apple takeover cash lined up

Why Steve Jobs chose the moral high ground over a hostile takeover

How Steve Jobs won control of Apple without owning it

Larry Ellison once had the money, the credit, and a fully worked-out plan to buy Apple outright. What he did not have was Steve Jobs' agreement. The Oracle co-founder revealed in a 2016 USC commencement speech that back in the mid-1990s, when Apple was worth roughly Rs 41,500 crore—about $5 billion—he had arranged to borrow every dollar needed to seize control of the struggling company and reinstall his close friend as CEO.The catch was Jobs himself. Ellison's pitch was blunt: buy Apple, hand Jobs the top job, done. But Jobs turned it down flat, telling Ellison he did not want power handed to him through a takeover. He wanted Apple to ask him back on its own terms—and he was not interested in the payday either."My idea was simple, buy Apple, and immediately make Steve CEO," Ellison said, recalling the plan. He pegged the price at around $5 billion and noted both men had strong credit. "I had already arranged to borrow all of the money. All Steve had to do was say yes." At the time Apple was hemorrhaging cash, shedding staff, and watching its stock sink to decade lows under CEO Gil Amelio. Wall Street had all but written it off.Jobs, then running NeXT after his 1985 ouster from Apple, saw a cleaner route. Rather than storm the company, he wanted Apple to acquire NeXT, ease him onto the board, and let the directors arrive at the obvious conclusion themselves.When Ellison pushed back with the operator's question—if they did not buy Apple, how would they make money—Jobs' reply became the line everyone remembers. "You don't need any more money," he told Ellison. "I'm not doing this for the money. I don't want to get paid." According to Walter Isaacson's biography, Jobs added that returning without either of them owning a stake would give him the moral high ground with employees and the industry.Ellison's retort was pure Ellison. He called it "the most expensive real estate on earth." A takeover, Jobs believed, would leave him legitimacy-poor in a company he needed to rally, and legitimacy mattered more to him than a controlling share.Jobs got his way. Apple bought NeXT in December 1996 for $429 million, brought Jobs in as an advisor, and by September 1997 he was interim CEO. The turnaround that followed—the iMac, the Microsoft investment truce, and eventually the iPhone—rebuilt Apple into the most valuable company on the planet, now worth well over $3.7 trillion.Ellison never got his takeover, but he got the outcome he actually wanted: Jobs back at the helm. He would join Apple's board that year and stay until 2002, stepping down when his schedule no longer allowed the commitment.
Article Summary: Larry ellison had $5 billion lined up to buy apple and make steve jobs ceo. Jobs turned it down flat, telling ellison he did not want power handed to him through a takeover. Ellison never got his takeover, but he got the outcome he actually wanted: jobs back at the helm.

Article Title: A memory shortage should scare a brand Lava's size, but MD Sunil Raina says it's an opening
Article Text:
Sunil Raina, Managing Director at Lava

A global memory shortage is pushing component costs up across the phone industry, squeezing exactly the mid-range tier where Lava lives. It should be a threat. Sunil Raina, Lava's managing director, has decided to treat it as an opening.His logic on the shortage is contrarian but clean. "The issue today is supply chain—component availability. Price is not the issue, because everybody is increasing prices. It's at parity," he says. A phone that sold at 10,000 rupees lands at 15,000; every brand moves together; the market shrinks a little. What was a 15,000-rupee spec sheet earlier now sits at 25,000 or 30,000. Nobody is spared, so nobody is undercut.That parity is where Raina sees room. "In our case, because our market share is not very high, the macro doesn't matter much," he says. "You're taking share from somebody." A brand sitting at two to three percent doesn't need the whole market to grow—it needs to pull buyers off someone bigger. Fifty percent growth is a mountain for the market and a molehill for Lava. Seventeen years in the business, he argues, has bought the supply-chain relationships to actually secure parts when they're scarce, and supply—not demand—is the only real constraint."If we get the supply, we'll do 50 percent or more. The first three months are already 40, 50 percent over last year."That discipline extends to what Lava won't do: chase the price umbrella upward faster than it has to. "I can't expect people to pay 250 just because I'm a new brand," Raina says of how he approaches any market—hold the spec-for-spec value even when costs are climbing, because the moment you break parity is the moment a rival takes the buyer back.It's a coherent bet, and an exposed one. The smallness that has always capped Lava is now the thing it's counting on—a low base that makes steep growth easier to claim, and a shortage that flattens the field just enough for a minor player to move. Whether that's strategy or the only move available is, as ever with Lava, a question of who you ask.
Article Summary: A global memory shortage is pushing component costs up across the phone industry. Sunil raina, lava's managing director, has decided to treat it as an opening. "in our case, because our market share is not very high, the macro doesn't matter much," he says.

Article Title: It's no country for freshers as IT firms revisit campus hiring playbook
Article Text:
Representative image

Bengaluru:It's no country for freshers.Campus offers are being delayed, onboarding schedules are being pushed back, and in some cases, roles are quietly withdrawn. Joining dates are increasingly tied to shifting business demand, leaving thousands of engineering graduates waiting months—sometimes over a year—for jobs they once assumed were guaranteed. For decades, a campus offer from an IT company came with an unwritten promise: graduate, join training and begin work. That certainty is beginning to disappear.The language companies are using reflects this new reality.IBM recently wrote to candidates waiting for an offer: "While we are trying to get you an early joining, there needs to be appropriate available demand to map you to that project. Once we have an update on the tentative date, our team will get in touch with you and initiate the offer process."At Accenture, after candidates applied for its Custom Software Engineer role, the company informed them: "Due to certain recent developments, we are no longer recruiting for this role." It added that candidates would be contacted if suitable openings matching their profile became available.At Oracle Financial Services Software (OFSS), candidates say offer letters have been delayed.At Cognizant, some GenC recruits have received repeated emails stating their joining date would depend on business requirements, successful background verification and delays linked to the EPFO's UAN portal.One email stated: "Please note that the EPFO portal is currently under maintenance and is expected to be resolved today. Based on the completion of your BGV formalities, the concerned team is working on revising your date of joining. We will keep you informed of any further updates."Another read: "Due to downtime on the UAN website, there may be a delay in processing. Your Date of Joining (DOJ) will be finalized based on business requirements and is subject to the successful completion of the Background Verification (BGV) process. We will keep you informed of further updates. Thank you for your patience and understanding."While administrative issues have contributed to some delays, the repeated references to "business requirements" illustrate how onboarding schedules have become increasingly fluid.The uncertainty has prompted freshers to create their own tracking tools. A 2025 Wipro recruit, frustrated by months of silence over onboarding, launched OnboardWatch, a crowdsourced website where candidates anonymously report whether their offers have been delayed, honoured or revoked.The anxiety comes amid a broader reset in the IT industry's hiring model.India's five largest IT services companies—TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCLTech and Tech Mahindra—collectively reduced their workforce by 6,981 employees in FY26, reversing net additions of 12,718 in FY25. TCS accounted for the biggest reduction, trimming 23,460 employees, while Tech Mahindra cut 1,108.Even as hiring slows at large IT firms, the nature of demand is changing. Nasscom estimates the technology industry's workforce grew by 1.35 lakh to 5.9 million in 2026, only marginally higher than the previous year's addition. Much of the incremental hiring is now coming from global capability centres (GCCs).Companies also say freshers require longer training before they become billable as enterprise technology becomes more complex.Namratha Dharshan, chief business leader at ISG, said opportunities for freshers remain, but employer expectations have changed. "Employers are increasingly prioritising specialised and deep-tech skills, with growing demand for talent that can work effectively with AI and emerging technologies rather than broad-based, generalist capabilities," she said.Phil Fersht, CEO of HFS Research, said the slowdown in campus onboarding reflects a structural shift in the industry's talent model. "Over the next three to five years, the traditional pyramid hiring model that powered India's IT services industry for decades will flatten considerably. Companies will recruit fewer people for generic delivery roles, but demand will increase for graduates with AI literacy, business domain knowledge, data engineering, cybersecurity and industry-specific expertise," he said.Even companies that continue to hire insist recruitment remains demand-led. TCS, which added more than 9,000 employees in the June quarter, says it continues to hire both experienced professionals and fresh graduates.Emails sent to IBM, Accenture, Oracle and Wipro seeking comment did not elicit a response till press time. Cognizant, however, said it remains committed to honouring all offers.Getting the offer, it seems, is no longer the same as getting the job.
Article Summary: Companies say freshers require longer training before they become billable as enterprise technology becomes more complex. India's five largest it services companies—tcs, infosys, wipro, hcltech and tech mahindra—collectively reduced their workforce by 6,981 employees in fy26.

Article Title: Netflix unveils ‘VOID’, an AI model that can change a movie plot
Article Text:
Netflix

How Netflix VOID works







Netflix has unveiled an AI model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, Netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next, essentially altering the causality. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background. VOID goes a step further: after erasing an object from a scene, it calculates how the surrounding environment should realistically respond to its absence, and in the process it adjusts the behaviour of other elements in the frame accordingly.The model, called VOID, is a vision-language system designed to edit video footage with a level of contextual awareness that sets it apart from several existing tools.For example, imagine this scenario: A movie director has just wrapped up a multi-million dollar action sequence where the star drives straight into a fiery, head-on collision. The cars explode, debris scatters, and the scene is a wrap.But suddenly, there is request to change the scene: The character survives and drive off into the sunset instead. This would mean costly reshoots or millions spent on computer-generated graphics.Netflix’s VOID can alter the scene and digitally erase the collision, the second vehicle, the smoke, and the flying debris. It will then generate new, realistic footage of pristine pavement, making it look as though the main character simply drove down an empty, peaceful road.The AI’s ‘magic’ lies in its grasp of real-world physics. Let us understand this by a different example. If a video shows a person jumping into a pool and splashing water onto the deck, VOID can completely erase the person. It will then intelligently alter the water and the wet concrete, leaving behind a perfectly still, undisturbed pool as if nobody had ever jumped in.“To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes,” Netflix said.“Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods,” the company added.Netflix isn’t keeping this powerful tool locked behind studio doors, and made the VOID model available to the public on the AI platform Hugging Face, allowing anyone to download and use it.While there are already several video-altering tools on the market, such as Runway, DiffuEraser, and ProPainter, the Netflix team claims VOID is vastly superior, as per The Register.
Article Summary: Netflix has unveiled an ai model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Mckinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Article Title: Hacker breaches S Korean database of nearly all diplomats
Article Text:
10,000 records of current and retired diplomats is believed to have been compromised

A system that held roughly 10,000 records of current and retired diplomats is believed to have been compromised by an unidentified hacker, foreign ministry spokesperson Park II told reporters on Tuesday. Park didn't confirm how many records had been accessed, but Yonhap News Agency reported that no sensitive data such as identification numbers, mobile phone numbers or home addresses appeared to have been affected."It appears that a significant amount of data has been leaked," Park said. "Govt is not ruling out any possibilities, including hacking organisations behind the scenes involving other countries."The ministry was notified by a relevant govt agency in early Feb about suspicious access to the online education system at the academy, Yonhap reported. The ministry shut down the system, which has remained offline since while an investigation is under way.South Korea is still reeling from a string of cybersecurity incidents, including a high-profile breach of Coupang, its leading online retailer. Last year, regulators discovered that a former employee improperly accessed personal information from nearly 34 million accounts, or about two-thirds of the country's population.North Korean hackers have carried out a series of cyberattacks in recent years, including the largest digital heist in the history of cryptocurrency in Feb last year.(This is a Bloomberg story)
Article Summary: A system that held roughly 10,000 records of current and retired diplomats is believed to have been compromised by an unidentified hacker. No sensitive data such as identification numbers, mobile phone numbers or home addresses appeared to be affected. South korea is still reeling from a string of cybersecurity incidents, including a high-profile breach of coupang.

Article Title: 'Are you the only Indian hire?' AI engineer denied B1 visa for company offsite, Redditors point out he has to prove why the meeting can't be held online
Article Text:
Indian AI engineer shares visa denial experience for company offsite in the US.

Getting a US visa for travel and business purposes has become extremely tricky as visa officers reject applications if they even have the slightest doubt that the applicant may not come back to their home country country; or start looking for a job in the US.An AI engineer shared his experience of B1 visa denial at the Delhi consulate on Reddit , as he gave his background that he works for a US-based startup from India. But his payroll and employment is through another company. The company's offsite is in October, for which he applied for a visa and got rejected. The Redditor shared how the conversation went.Officer: What's your job title?Redditor: AI Engineer.Officer: Where do you work?Redditor: I work for Company A. My employment in India is through Company B, which handles my payroll.Officer: Why do you need to go to the US? What do you do?Redditor: I'm an AI Engineer working on AI workflow automation software. This trip is for our engineering offsite. During the meeting, we'll discuss business priorities for the next quarter, and since I'm one of the early/core engineering team members, I'm required to participate in engineering planning and technical decisions.Officer: So it's your own business?Redditor: No, I'm an employee.Officer: How long have you been with the company?Redditor: Since September last year.Officer: Are you the only Indian hire?Redditor: Yes.Officer: And you're the only one traveling?Redditor: Yes. I'm the only employee traveling from India. We're a remote company with employees in other countries like Mexico and Canada, so team members from those locations will also be attending.After this conversation, the man was handed a 214(b) refusal slip, which means the applicant does not have enough ties to his home country, but the man said that he was never asked about his family members and he could have proved that he has his mother, wife and a property under his name in India.Redditors pointed out that since he works for a US company, it raises a red flag for visa officers, as he could not prove why he needed to attend the offsite, as the meeting can also be held online.
